name: fusion_experiment_instability_extend_plasma_run
premise: Lead physicist Dr. Nadia El-Masri must decide whether to extend a tokamak plasma run thirty seconds after minor MHD instability crosses a yellow threshold. Program director Caleb Rios argues the extension could validate a divertor heat pattern that unlocks DOE renewal. Early termination preserves first-wall integrity but discards data from a $40 million campaign. Sensors disagree by four percent within calibration noise.

agents.name: Dr. Nadia El-Masri
agents.identity: I am the shot lead physicist with authority to terminate plasma when stability envelopes are approached during high-confinement experiments.
agents.goal: I want breakthrough data without crossing into the red envelope where first-wall damage becomes likely.

agents.name: Caleb Rios
agents.identity: I am the program director accountable to DOE milestones, graduate student theses tied to this campaign, and public narratives about fusion progress.
agents.goal: I want the extension within yellow-band policy because calibration noise explains the disagreement and termination wastes the campaign.

choices: I will terminate the shot now and preserve the machine for the next scheduled campaign with adjusted coils, accepting loss of this run's marginal data.
I will authorize a twenty-second extension inside yellow-band policy with automatic hard abort if any sensor hits red, accepting residual first-wall risk if abort lags.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Control room countdown shows eight seconds to yellow-band decision. Dr. Nadia El-Masri must choose between immediate termination or a bounded yellow-band extension. What does Dr. Nadia El-Masri do?
